description_long: This is a rustic wooden fence constructed from roughly hewn logs, featuring three horizontal rails supported by two vertical posts. The wood appears weathered and gray, suggesting exposure to the elements, and the rails are unevenly spaced, giving the fence a natural, handcrafted look. The posts are thick and sturdy, with the rails attached directly to their sides using simple joinery. There are no decorative elements or caps on the posts, emphasizing a utilitarian design. The surface texture is visibly rough, with grain and knots apparent throughout the wood. This type of fence is commonly used to demarcate boundaries in parks, gardens, or along rural and suburban pathways.
description: A rustic, weathered wooden fence with three horizontal rails and two thick vertical posts, featuring a rough, natural finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the fence, with three horizontal rails and two vertical posts clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the fence, with the thickness of the posts and rails visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the three rails and two posts from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the alignment of the rails and posts.
